channel.title: Spiral Threads :: CS Two Year Refactor

item.title: Computational Models & Error Handling
item.description: Thread: Computational Models & Error Handling Working draft for faculty review. A spiral deepens through escalating passes across blocks. This is the flagship spiral; one of nine spirals (alongside three lenses and one bounded practice). In one line: From naming that there’s more than one model, to comparing production fault-tolerance philosophies — with ‘how does this model handle failure’ as the connective idea. Character FLAGSHIP. Merges two directives (computational models seeded throughout; errors as normative, compared across models) because how a model handles failure is one of its most important properties. Genuinely uncommon undergraduate content — most programs teach try/catch as the only model.

item.title: Data Structures & Representation
item.description: Thread: Data Structures & Representation Working draft for faculty review. A spiral deepens through escalating passes across blocks. This is the spiral; one of nine spirals (alongside three lenses and one bounded practice). In one line: From the most primitive representation to the most general, each pass a genuine generalization of the last. Character The strongest spiral in the design. Each pass is a real generalization, not a harder repeat. After B3 it is taught contract-first (ADT interface before implementation), which is what lets B4 ask ‘how do we choose among implementations of the same contract?’

item.title: Algorithmic Thinking & Complexity
item.description: Thread: Algorithmic Thinking & Complexity Working draft for faculty review. A spiral deepens through escalating passes across blocks. This is the spiral; one of nine spirals (alongside three lenses and one bounded practice). In one line: From Big-O as theory, to graph optimization, to a real bottleneck under load. Character Moved its first pass from B3 to B4 so it can answer the question B3’s contract-first ADTs raise: how do you choose among implementations of the same contract? Closely shadowed by the Optimization lens.

item.title: Program Correctness / Code Comprehension
item.description: Thread: Program Correctness / Code Comprehension Working draft for faculty review. A spiral deepens through escalating passes across blocks. This is the spiral; one of nine spirals (alongside three lenses and one bounded practice). In one line: From ‘make it work’ to ‘defend it under scrutiny’ — the lens changes each pass. Character Each pass shifts the lens rather than just raising difficulty: produce → understand others’ → organize for correctness → verify → defend.

item.title: HCI / Accessible & Human-Centered Design
item.description: Thread: HCI / Accessible & Human-Centered Design Working draft for faculty review. A spiral deepens through escalating passes across blocks. This is the spiral; one of nine spirals (alongside three lenses and one bounded practice). In one line: From accessibility-checklist compliance to requirements-driven design — carrying TWO embedded throughlines (accessibility + data visualization). Character Carries two throughlines that belong together (both are about communicating to a human effectively and honestly). The key transition is B2’s checklist compliance → B8’s designing from others’ needs outward.

item.title: Boundaries & Contracts
item.description: Thread: Boundaries & Contracts Working draft for faculty review. A spiral deepens through escalating passes across blocks. This is the spiral (meta-thread); one of nine spirals (alongside three lenses and one bounded practice). In one line: A promise about behavior at a boundary, independent of what’s hidden behind it — escalating by the KIND of boundary the contract spans. Character Both a spiral and a META-thread: it names the common structure beneath APIs, schemas, trust boundaries, versioning, and verification. Arguably the strongest expression of the program’s systems-thinking ethos. Each pass introduces a new kind of boundary.

item.title: APIs & Networks
item.description: Thread: APIs & Networked Systems Working draft for faculty review. A spiral deepens through escalating passes across blocks. This is the spiral; one of nine spirals (alongside three lenses and one bounded practice). In one line: Consume → build → integrate multiple → operate in production. Character A clean four-pass escalation in what the student does with a networked boundary. Passes across the two years Block Host context What’s new at this pass B2 (Y1 Fall) CS-106 Web Foundations Consume a basic, read-only API. B4 (Y1 Spring) CS-111 Systems & APIs Build and consume your own API; system structure across a network boundary. B7 (Y2 Spring) CS-207 Data Integration Integrate multiple sources — combine, not just call. B8 (Y2 Spring) CS-208 Deployment & Operations Operate a live service in production. Connections The network boundary is the B4 pass of Boundaries & Contracts. If OSI Networking Fundamentals is adopted, it becomes the theoretical underpinning this practical thread applies. Open questions for faculty review OSI Networking Fundamentals placement (and its credits) remain deferred to cross-block optimization; it would enrich this thread’s early passes. For how this lands in a specific block, see that block’s review file.

item.title: Git & Version Control
item.description: Thread: Git & Version Control Working draft for faculty review. A spiral deepens through escalating passes across blocks. This is the spiral; one of nine spirals (alongside three lenses and one bounded practice). In one line: Read history → commit/revert safety net → solo branching → collaborative workflow. Character Redesigned from a single late pass into four. Version control as ’tracking how things change over time’ is introduced as a READING tool first, before it is a workflow tool.

item.title: Sociotechncial Structure
item.description: Working draft for faculty review. A spiral deepens through escalating passes across blocks. This is the spiral; one of nine spirals (alongside three lenses and one bounded practice). In one line: Systems and the humans who build them are one structural fabric — from observing the parallel, to living it, to reflecting on it. Character Models the BUILDERS structurally, not just the users — extending the notional machine’s top layer. Seeded conceptually, then lived through the team project, then reflected on. Complements the Git collaboration thread (Git is the tool; this is the social reality the tool serves).
